title: Living in Boulder blog 8 - 17 - 2017: 39.5 miles 3:38:38 Time  4,869ft

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps a bike ride in the Boulder area covering 39.5 miles and 4,869 feet of climbing, describing the route through Jamestown, Poor Man road, Farmout, Wall Street, and Gold Hill. He shares observations about an old mining town that housed 300 people and operated from 1902–1905, then briefly mentions working on a book project cataloging ancient world and church father figures, and experimenting with Strava lap features on his bike computer.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Acknowledges he is not yet fit enough for these rides and needs to avoid overexerting himself
Eager to work up to all-day rides and potentially a 100-mile challenge
Finds it rewarding but strange that 30-mile rides feel hard compared to when he was super fit
Very motivated and thinks he can finish it soon by zooming through shorter fragments

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The platforms for the houses and an old stage were still up there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He saw wagon wells up there.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The estate is offices of Museum open from 11 to 3 every third Saturday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He went up to the end of the road and went back down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He tried to imagine the town being there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has already looked at the area on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Riding is amazing after looking at the doors because it's like going inside of the door.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He went up to gold hill and then down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It was getting dark when he got home.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thought he would just want to get on the scale.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was out of water.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is a water guzzler because he's just starting.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His ride was 38 miles and 4800 feet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's fun to ride but he needs to make sure he's not overdoing it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is struggling because it's more painful than it would be if he were super fit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's weird to think of a short 30-mile ride being really hard.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can't wait till he can do a ride starting in the morning and go all day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There's a strawberry challenge to do a hundred-mile ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He came home and was working on YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is working his way down one page to capture everyone from the ancient world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to make sure he gets everybody from the ancient world and the church fathers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are a lot of fragments and short ones.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thinks he can zoom through and finish soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is super motivated.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was looking on Strava and figuring around Strava.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: With his bike computer, he can hit laps to get information between the times he hits it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is using Strava's segment feature to know the watts and other data.
